variables.description: Select the method for handling SSL certificates. 'self-signed' is the recommended default for local networks and requires no extra setup. 'lets-encrypt' provides a trusted certificate but requires you to own a domain name and forward port 80 from your router to this device.
variables.label: Certificate Method

variables.description: Your email address, used for Let's Encrypt registration and recovery.
variables.label: Let's Encrypt Admin Email

variables.description: The external port to access the Traefik dashboard (e.g., 8080).
variables.label: Dashboard Port

variables.description: Logging verbosity. Options: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR.
variables.label: Log Level

variables.description: Host directory for storing Let's Encrypt SSL certificates.
variables.label: ACME Storage Path

variables.description: Host directory for Traefik's dynamic configuration files.
variables.label: Config Path

variables.description: Your public domain name for dashboard access (e.g., traefik.your.domain). Required for Let's Encrypt.
variables.label: Domain Name

variables.description: SECURITY CRITICAL: A 'user:hashed_password' pair for dashboard access. You MUST use a tool like 'htpasswd' to generate a compatible md5 hash (apr1 format). The default value is 'admin:password'. For production, generate a new hash, store it in your .env file (e.g., TRAEFIK_USERS_HASH=...), and reference it here using the macro: {{ DOTENV.TRAEFIK_USERS_HASH }}.
variables.label: Dashboard Credentials
